title: A computed, aliased column
prompt_md:
APIs often return derived/computed fields, not raw columns. For every showtime, return the screen letter **uppercased** (it already is, but make it explicit and deterministic) and the **length** of the movie's title.

Join `showtimes` to `movies`. Return three columns:

- `movie` — the movie's `title`
- `screen` — `UPPER(showtimes.screen)`
- `title_len` — `length(movies.title)` (an integer)
starter_code:
SELECT m.title AS movie,
       UPPER(s.screen) AS screen,
       length(m.title) AS title_len
FROM showtimes s
JOIN movies m ON s.movie_id = m.id;
hints: `length('Dune')` is 4; `UPPER('a')` is 'A'. Alias each output with `AS`.
reference_solution: SELECT m.title AS movie, UPPER(s.screen) AS screen, length(m.title) AS title_len FROM showtimes s JOIN movies m ON s.movie_id = m.id;
